text:
BILATERAL BREAST MRI WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST (NEOADJUVANT TREATMENT RESPONSE ASSESSMENT)

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Ms. Marisol Fuentes-Delgado is a 58-year-old Hispanic postmenopausal woman with recently diagnosed left breast triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma, grade 3, Stage IIB (T2N1M0) with known left axillary nodal involvement. Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant confirmed (c.5266dupC). She is currently undergoing neoadjuvant chemotherapy with dose-dense doxorubicin/cyclophosphamide (ddAC) in combination with pembrolizumab (KEYNOTE-522 protocol). This MRI is obtained for mid-treatment response assessment following completion of 4 cycles of ddAC and concurrent pembrolizumab. Baseline tumor size was 3.4 cm on diagnostic ultrasound with one suspicious left axillary lymph node. Prior staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and bone scan demonstrated no evidence of distant metastatic disease.

TECHNIQUE:
Bilateral breast MRI performed at 1.5 Tesla using a dedicated bilateral breast coil. Sequences obtained include: sagittal T2-weighted fat-saturated images bilaterally, axial T1-weighted pre-contrast and dynamic post-contrast series (4 phases at 90-second intervals) following intravenous administration of 0.1 mmol/kg gadobutrol (Gadavist), subtraction images, and maximum intensity projection (MIP) reconstructions. Diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with ADC mapping performed bilaterally. CAD analysis performed. No adverse reaction to contrast material.

COMPARISON:
Diagnostic mammogram and targeted breast ultrasound from prior to initiation of therapy (index imaging). No prior breast MRI available for direct comparison.

---

FINDINGS:

LEFT BREAST:

Primary Tumor Site:
In the upper central left breast at approximately the 12 o'clock position, roughly 4 cm posterior to the nipple, there is a residual area of irregular heterogeneous enhancement measuring approximately 1.6 x 1.4 x 1.5 cm in the axial and sagittal planes (previously measured at 3.4 x 2.9 x 3.1 cm on diagnostic ultrasound at initial presentation). This represents a substantial reduction from the baseline lesion -- in the range of approximately 50-55% linear reduction of the dominant dimension. The enhancement pattern demonstrates persistent irregular margins with heterogeneous internal enhancement. On kinetic analysis, the dominant kinetic curve type within the residual lesion demonstrates a mixed pattern with portions showing plateau kinetics and other areas with early washout, which may indicate a mixture of viable residual tumor and treatment-related fibrotic change. The T2-weighted signal within this region is mildly elevated and heterogeneous, consistent with admixed edema, fibrosis, and possible residual cellular tumor. On diffusion-weighted imaging, there remains a focus of restricted diffusion within the enhancing region (ADC approximately 0.82 x 10^-3 mm^2/s), slightly above the pre-treatment nadir (ADC values not available for direct comparison from baseline, but restricted diffusion persists), consistent with residual viable tumor.

There is no evidence of skin thickening or skin enhancement at the overlying dermis compared to the baseline examination. The pectoralis major muscle and the anterior chest wall show no involvement. No new enhancing satellite nodules are identified within the left breast. The previously noted peau d'orange type skin edema seen at the 12 o'clock position on baseline imaging is no longer appreciated.

There is a broad region of nonspecific background parenchymal enhancement (BPE) in the left breast at a low to moderate degree, which is expected in the post-treatment setting and somewhat complicates interpretation; however, the dominant residual lesion is still clearly delineated.

No new areas of suspicious enhancement are identified elsewhere in the left breast. No additional satellite lesions or multicentric foci of enhancement are identified in the left breast.

LEFT AXILLA:
In the left axillary region, a lymph node is identified measuring approximately 1.0 cm in short axis (previously reported as 1.8 cm with 8 mm cortical thickening and loss of fatty hilum on baseline ultrasound). The current appearance shows a partially reconstituted fatty hilum and less cortical thickening (now approximately 3-4 mm) compared to the baseline examination. The residual cortical thickening cannot be definitively attributed to treatment effect versus residual micrometastatic involvement on imaging alone, and remains indeterminate. No additional abnormal left axillary lymph nodes are identified. No suspicious level II or level III axillary lymph nodes.

LEFT INTERNAL MAMMARY REGION:
No pathologically enlarged or suspiciously enhancing lymph nodes are identified along the left internal mammary chain.

RIGHT BREAST:
No suspicious enhancement, mass, or architectural distortion identified in the right breast. No axillary lymphadenopathy on the right. Background parenchymal enhancement is symmetric and low. No suspicious lesion identified. The right breast appears entirely within normal limits for a postmenopausal patient of this age.

RIGHT AXILLA:
No abnormal lymph nodes identified in the right axilla.

SKIN AND NIPPLE-AREOLAR COMPLEX:
Left nipple-areolar complex: No retraction, enhancement, or involvement identified. Right nipple-areolar complex: Normal. Skin bilaterally: No focal skin thickening or enhancement.

CHEST WALL:
Bilateral pectoralis major and minor muscles appear intact. No chest wall involvement identified on either side.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. LEFT BREAST PRIMARY TUMOR -- PARTIAL TREATMENT RESPONSE: The dominant left breast lesion at the 12 o'clock position demonstrates a marked reduction in size from the pre-treatment baseline, with the residual enhancing component now measuring approximately 1.6 x 1.4 cm (down from 3.4 cm at initial diagnosis by ultrasound). This represents an approximately 50-55% reduction in the dominant dimension, indicative of a meaningful partial response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy with ddAC and pembrolizumab. Residual irregular enhancement with mixed kinetics and persistent restricted diffusion suggests viable residual tumor is present within this region, though treatment-related fibrosis and stromal changes cannot be excluded without histologic correlation.

2. LEFT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE -- INDETERMINATE, POSSIBLY RESPONDING: The previously suspicious left axillary lymph node now measures approximately 1.0 cm with partial reconstitution of the fatty hilum. This morphologic evolution is consistent with treatment response, though radiographic normalization is incomplete. Residual nodal involvement cannot be excluded on imaging alone.

3. NO NEW SITES OF DISEASE: No new enhancing lesions in either breast, no new suspicious axillary or internal mammary adenopathy, and no chest wall involvement identified.

4. RIGHT BREAST: No suspicious findings. Normal postmenopausal appearance.

Overall, the imaging findings are consistent with an ongoing partial radiographic response to neoadjuvant systemic therapy. The residual enhancing mass with restricted diffusion warrants continued clinical and imaging follow-up. Definitive pathologic assessment of treatment response will be required at the time of surgical resection.

Reporting Radiologist: [Electronically Signed]
Section of Breast Imaging, Department of Radiology
[Institution]
ACR BI-RADS Category: 6 (Known biopsy-proven malignancy -- treatment monitoring study)
